title: Job Vacancies at JMG
content:
JMG is a diversified solution provider with a broad portfolio in power generation, electrical infrastructures, industrial equipment, elevators & escalators and HVAC solutions. With 20 years of experience in Africa and strong partnerships with some of the world’s leading brands, JMG combines global technologies and regional expertise to fulfill the development needs of the African market.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Field Sales Representative (Solar Wholesales)

Job Locations: Abuja (FCT) and Kaduna
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a motivated and experienced Sales Representative to drive sales and expand our market presence for our brand.
	The ideal candidate will have strong knowledge of solar inverters, batteries, or related energy systems, a proven ability to establish dealerships, and experience driving B2B sales.
	This role is key to expanding our market presence and building strategic distribution and partner networks.


Duties & Responsibilities


	Develop and execute a growth-focused sales strategy for solar inverters and batteries in the wholesale and B2B space.
	Identify and onboard new dealers, distributors, and channel partners across key regions.
	Build and manage relationships with EPCs, solar installers, commercial clients, and institutional buyers.
	Analyze market trends and competitor activity to uncover new opportunities and maintain a competitive edge.


Requirements


	B.Eng / HND in Engineering, Renewable Energy, or a related field.
	3-5 years of experience in technical or industrial sales, preferably in the solar, electrical, or energy storage sectors.
	Strong knowledge of solar inverters, lithium batteries, and off-grid or hybrid power systems.
	Demonstrated ability to develop dealership networks and B2B partnerships.


Application Closing Date: 14th July, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Resume to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

2.) Senior Sales Executive - Technical Sales (B2B)

Job Locations: Abuja (FCT) and Kano
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Senior Sales Executive is responsible for driving sales of generators (diesel and gas) to businesses, industries, government institutions, and individual clients.
	This role involves identifying new business opportunities, maintaining client relationships, and achieving sales targets.
	The ideal candidate should have a strong technical understanding of power generation systems and a proven track record in B2B and B2G sales.


Duties & Responsibilities


	Generate and close new business opportunities in industrial and commercial markets
	Build and maintain strong client relationships
	Conduct client visits, presentations, and product demos
	Negotiate contracts and achieve set revenue targets
	Stay informed on market trends, competitors, and customer needs, including manufacturing, construction, automotive, and more.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, or Engineering
	Minimum of 3 years of experience in generator or industrial equipment sales
	Strong technical knowledge of diesel/gas generators
	Proven B2B/B2G sales track record
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and presentation skills
	Must Be Resident in Kano or Abuja.


Application Closing Date: 18th July, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

3.) Store Account Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Brief Description


	Posting all Installation Job and Engineer transfers


Duties


	Handle all store related entries and postings on Microsoft Dynamics AX such as: Installation Jobs and Engineer transfers
	Perform periodic accounts and reconciliation to ensure that warehouses are balanced.
	Prepare transfers and journals.
	Carry out daily reporting and supervision of store activities; pending deliveries and sales orders.
	Coordinate activities between stores and production departments.
	Maintain proper documentation of all transactions.
	Do proper filing on daily basis for all posted transactions.
	Take up IMS responsibilities such as reporting unsafe act, condition or procedure in the work place, participating in fire or emergency drill at work, participating in incident reporting and investigation when necessary and adherence to Company policies.


Minimum Requirements


	BSc / BA in Accounting, Finance or equivalent.
	1-3 years of relevant working experience will be a plus. Having CPA, CFA, CMA or any other certification is a plus.


Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of accounting and financial reporting principles and practices
	Team player
	Strong communication skills
	Flexible
	Great attention to detail
	Ability to work under pressure
	Deadline-Oriented
	Trustworthy and dependable.


Application Closing Date: 18th July, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Administrator",
  "description": "Outsource Nigeria is a full-service Human Resources agency, specializing in providing outsourced turnkey manpower solutions to growing companies. Our solution include HR Services, Recruiting, Accounting, IT Support, Business Intelligence, Developers, and Operations including management and field / phone / automated based resources. The core of Outsource Nigeria&rsquo;s success is its people.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Administrator

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Operations Administrator is responsible for providing administrative and operational support to ensure the smooth and efficient running of the organization&#39;s daily activities.
	The role involves coordinating operational processes, maintaining records, preparing reports, managing schedules, and liaising with internal departments and external stakeholders to enhance productivity and service delivery.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate and support day-to-day operational activities.
	Maintain accurate operational records, files, and documentation.
	Prepare weekly and monthly operational reports.
	Monitor office supplies and initiate procurement requests when necessary.
	Schedule meetings, appointments, and operational activities.
	Assist in developing and implementing operational procedures and policies.
	Track departmental budgets, expenses, and invoices.
	Liaise with vendors, service providers, and external partners.
	Ensure compliance with organizational policies and regulatory requirements.
	Support inventory management and asset tracking processes.
	Handle correspondence, emails, and telephone inquiries related to operations.
	Coordinate travel arrangements and logistics when required.
	Monitor deadlines and follow up on pending operational tasks.
	Assist in onboarding new employees by coordinating operational requirements.
	Identify process improvement opportunities and recommend solutions.
	Perform any other duties assigned by management.


KPIs


	Timeliness and accuracy of operational reports.
	Efficiency in coordinating operational activities.
	Compliance with established procedures and policies.
	Reduction in administrative errors.
	Effective record management and documentation.
	Timely completion of assigned tasks.
	Internal stakeholder satisfaction.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or Higher National Diploma HND/B.SC in Business Administration, Operations Management, Management, Accounting, or any related field.
	1&ndash;3 years of proven experience in an administrative or operations role.
	Professional certifications in Administration or Operations Management will be an added advantage.
	Experience using office management systems and procedures.
	Previous experience in a fast-paced environment is preferred.


Core Competencies:


	Strong organizational and multitasking skills.
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Attention to detail and accuracy.
	Time management and ability to meet deadlines.
	Problem-solving and analytical thinking.
	Ability to prioritize tasks effectively.
	Teamwork and interpersonal skills.
	High level of integrity and confidentiality.
	Proactive and results-oriented approach.
	Adaptability and flexibility.


Technical Skills:


	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook).
	Knowledge of Google Workspace applications.
	Experience with data entry and report preparation.
	Familiarity with ERP or operations management software is an advantage.
	Basic understanding of budgeting and inventory control.


Working Conditions:


	Standard office working hours.
	May occasionally require extended hours to meet operational demands.
	Collaborative work environment involving multiple departments.


Salary &amp; Benefits


	N150,000 monthly
	Performance-based incentives and bonuses
	HMO/Medical Insurance
	Career Growth Opportunities.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: jobs@outsourcenigeria.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Operations Administrator Job at Outsource Nigeria




        
    

        
        Outsource Nigeria is a full-service Human Resources agency, specializing in providing outsourced turnkey manpower solutions to growing companies. Our solution include HR Services, Recruiting, Accounting, IT Support, Business Intelligence, Developers, and Operations including management and field / phone / automated based resources. The core of Outsource Nigeria’s success is its people.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Administrator

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Operations Administrator is responsible for providing administrative and operational support to ensure the smooth and efficient running of the organization's daily activities.
	The role involves coordinating operational processes, maintaining records, preparing reports, managing schedules, and liaising with internal departments and external stakeholders to enhance productivity and service delivery.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate and support day-to-day operational activities.
	Maintain accurate operational records, files, and documentation.
	Prepare weekly and monthly operational reports.
	Monitor office supplies and initiate procurement requests when necessary.
	Schedule meetings, appointments, and operational activities.
	Assist in developing and implementing operational procedures and policies.
	Track departmental budgets, expenses, and invoices.
	Liaise with vendors, service providers, and external partners.
	Ensure compliance with organizational policies and regulatory requirements.
	Support inventory management and asset tracking processes.
	Handle correspondence, emails, and telephone inquiries related to operations.
	Coordinate travel arrangements and logistics when required.
	Monitor deadlines and follow up on pending operational tasks.
	Assist in onboarding new employees by coordinating operational requirements.
	Identify process improvement opportunities and recommend solutions.
	Perform any other duties assigned by management.


KPIs


	Timeliness and accuracy of operational reports.
	Efficiency in coordinating operational activities.
	Compliance with established procedures and policies.
	Reduction in administrative errors.
	Effective record management and documentation.
	Timely completion of assigned tasks.
	Internal stakeholder satisfaction.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree or Higher National Diploma HND/B.SC in Business Administration, Operations Management, Management, Accounting, or any related field.
	1–3 years of proven experience in an administrative or operations role.
	Professional certifications in Administration or Operations Management will be an added advantage.
	Experience using office management systems and procedures.
	Previous experience in a fast-paced environment is preferred.


Core Competencies:


	Strong organizational and multitasking skills.
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Attention to detail and accuracy.
	Time management and ability to meet deadlines.
	Problem-solving and analytical thinking.
	Ability to prioritize tasks effectively.
	Teamwork and interpersonal skills.
	High level of integrity and confidentiality.
	Proactive and results-oriented approach.
	Adaptability and flexibility.


Technical Skills:


	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook).
	Knowledge of Google Workspace applications.
	Experience with data entry and report preparation.
	Familiarity with ERP or operations management software is an advantage.
	Basic understanding of budgeting and inventory control.


Working Conditions:


	Standard office working hours.
	May occasionally require extended hours to meet operational demands.
	Collaborative work environment involving multiple departments.


Salary & Benefits


	N150,000 monthly
	Performance-based incentives and bonuses
	HMO/Medical Insurance
	Career Growth Opportunities.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Personal Assistant Job at TeamAce Limited




        
    

        
        TeamAce Limited - Our client, a Non-Governmental Organization (NGO), is recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Personal Assistant

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Manage and coordinate the executive's calendar, appointments, meetings, and travel arrangements.
	Prepare reports, presentations, correspondence, and meeting minutes.
	Act as the primary point of contact between the executive and internal/external stakeholders.
	Organize and maintain confidential records, documents, and filing systems.
	Track projects, deadlines, and action items to ensure timely execution.
	Support the planning and coordination of meetings, events, workshops, and stakeholder engagements.
	Conduct research and compile information required for decision-making and reporting.
	Assist in drafting proposals, reports, and other organizational documents.
	Handle administrative and operational tasks as assigned by management.
	Ensure efficient communication and follow-up with partners, donors, and other stakeholders.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Social Sciences, Humanities, or a related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience as a Personal Assistant, Executive Assistant, or Administrative Officer.
	Prior experience working in an NGO is highly preferred.
	Excellent organizational and time management skills.
	Strong written and verbal communication skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook).
	Ability to multitask and manage competing priorities effectively.
	High level of professionalism, discretion, and confidentiality.
	Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills.


Key Competencies:


	Attention to detail
	Strong organizational skills
	Effective communication
	Problem-solving ability
	Time management
	Stakeholder management
	Confidentiality and professionalism.


Salary
N250,000 - N350,000 per month.     

    

        Click here to Apply
